title: Nutrition: Vitamins and Minerals
content:
ACCESSORY FACTORS

Fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K): Body can store.

Water-soluble vitamins (B complex, C): Must be continually replaced. Lost through indifferent handling, excessive processing, poor cooking.

Minerals: Save vegetable cooking waters for soups/sauces to retain.

Daily recommendations:
- Two 3-ounce servings complete-protein foods (meat, fish, fowl, eggs), or cereal-legume combinations with complete protein added
- 2 cups fresh milk or equivalent (1 cup milk = 1 cup ice cream = ½ cup cottage cheese = 1-inch cube cheddar)
- 4+ servings starchy foods (baked goods, cereals, pastas) accenting whole grains
- 4+ ½-¾ cup servings fruits and vegetables including citrus/tomatoes and dark green/deep yellow vegetables (one raw leafy green preferred)
- Variety for complete nutrition

Fluid intake: 5-7 glasses daily including water.

Iodized salt: Use in regions where water/soil lack iodine.
